mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 55% of instances when UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand that drives sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle confirms resilient economic output that offsets higher discount rates, producing positive short-term equity returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands corporate revenue baselines and earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates coincide with negative 60-day forward returns in over 50% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 55% of instances during inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that supports consumer confidence and drives positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp quarterly declines in housing starts during periods of financial stress signal collapsing credit conditions and reduced construction activity that trigger negative short-term equity returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 5% during financial stress periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60% of cases.
